channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1 — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-07 07:51 UTC

OP

Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1

Early replies (first 3)

61934693
jasmy

61935114
>>61934344
10m jasmy daimyo here
2 words
Apple partnership
I've been stacking since 2023

61935154
based

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61935550
ohh jasmy

61935609
>>61935597
uhhhhhh what is this

61935646
>>61935609
>Jasmy now has a compliant domestic payment rail through its collaboration with Aplix Corporation, which was licensed by Japan’s Financial Services Agency to issue electronic money. This doesn’t confirm that JPYD is launched or that it is a bank-backed stablecoin, but it establishes the legal infrastructure needed to build and deploy regulated digital payments (wallets, merchant payments, local government programs, etc.) that could later integrate blockchain settlement. In short: Jasmy gains regulatory groundwork for real payment infrastructure in Japan, but the exact structure of JPYD is still unknown.

61935684
>>61935646
sounds interesting especially considering mcap is low rn
would this actually go to the token though? I thought the token was supposed to be for iot? I don't even remember anymore it's been years since I sold.

61936773
JASMY

61937984
>>61935114
>>61935684
good questions. Is apple buying jasms or not?

61938015
>>61935114
AI chatbots tell me that big companies are using Jasmy for "data lockers"... where is the evidence they are buying and pumping price?

61938024
(You) vill buy ze top

61938468
gm

61939466
yes jasmy

61941110
hennlo

61942093
hey

61943184
hi snail

61946007
heloo>>61941110

61948636
day 5 of snailbumping every day until the 'log is nothing but snailposting

item.title: BAT bros I don't feel so good — 41 replies — 164.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BAT bros I don't feel so good

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-03-07 07:54 UTC

OP

Eich scammed us I just hope we get back to 20c so I can dump this shit

Early replies (first 3)

61942645
Nigga you have to wait a full fucking year. It dumps consistently in December-January, so buy now and dump then.

The eich nigger will never ever employ actual use cases for this token because he and his team are scared little faggots.

The president and his entire family have publicly rug pulled multiple times and nothing has come of it negatively for them. Brendan eich is just a scared little faggot do nothing bitch boy.

61942696
>>61942058
>>61942645
I solded after I saw Eich namedropped in the epstein files.

61942728
>>61942058
Who would've thought 27 cents would be the local top for BAT

the highs get lower

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61946835
does no one know what they are working on? i guess if you know u know

61946916
>>61943410
Still here and still holding a good amount of BAT, but I've dumped a lot.

>>61944901
>but sunk cost fallacy is real
It really is ;_;

>>61946835
Only thing I've heard lately is Brave Origin, but I'm not really around or looking into Brave/BAT like I used to.

61947061
>>61942696
Oh shit, was he really?

>>61943276
Sorry about your losses, lad. Hope you recouped.

61947085
>>61946916
you know what else you can dump? these webms

61947107
>>61943410
Since May 2017

61947151
>>61947061
who the fuck has washing machine in the kitchen. What tasteless whore. Let me guess, toilet bowl is in the bedroom?

61947166
>>61947085
Make a request, lad. I'll check every once in a while between my vidya grinding.

>>61947107
Based OG.

>>61947151
Based reposter. I dropped that one a few threads ago.

61947258
>>61946916
>>61947166
HOLY FUCKKKKK
got any big nigress booba

61947261
As shitty as bat is the community has always been solid

61947270
>>61944901
>>61946916
You're standing in front of a burning pile of money that you refuse to extinguish on the off chance that burning your money somehow makes you richer?

61947278
>>61947166
>>61947151
>>61947061
>>61946916
based thot poster

61947296
>>61943085
>hope Eich dumps it to zero, replaces BAT with stablecoins, and they get every use case in Brave you bitched about having.
The rational way to do this is Brendan launches his own stablefuck and he pays dividends with BAT. Problem is, he’s too smart to get in over his head with crapto. He was mentioned in the Epstein files but they couldn’t get him on the island. We’re not dealing with a retard like Brock Pierce or a based patriot like Nikolai from MKR. Brendan survives because he’s not too greedy or too political other his absolute hatred of faggots

61947372
>>61947296
Spoken like a terminal baggie. That's not rational at all. Make his own stablecoin so he can get raped by American finance? Then pay dividends in BAT so he can get raped again? Dividends for what? Paid to who? Why would a browser company get a banking license and why would they pay dividends instead of charge a fee so they can make a profit for once.

61947466
>>61947166
some gifted TnA in a razor thin bikini

61947742
>>61947258
I got you next post. Light or dark skinned?

>>61947270
I've been selling off a lot of BAT recently. Not at these prices though. I'll probably keep a good amount just because I'm that retarded.

I have other investments, I get a solid salary, and I have several income streams. Does it suck to lose out holding BAT? Sure, but it is what it is.

>>61947466
Like this? If not or you want a different body type, be as specific as you want.

61947792
>>61946916
its something else entirely. its big.

61947892
>>61947792
I'll take the bait for the sake of hopium kek. Even if something big is coming, its likely far too late desu.

>>61947466
Here's another for you.

>>61947278
Based fellow OG who missed early retirement holding this piece of SHIT.

61947959
>>61946916
>>61947166
>>61947742
>>61947892
YOUR CONTENT IS AMAZING

61947966
>>61947892
it depends on what that thing is. in this case, i wouldnt say its necessarily too late. i guess we'll see.

61948112
>>61947892
Moar please

61948155
>>61946916
>the perfect ass does not exist

61948157
>>61947966
What can you tell us?

61948171
>>61947892
>Based fellow OG who missed early retirement holding this piece of SHIT

Doesn't feel very based but I'm glad there are at least a few of us suffering together

also, requesting my threadly dose of big booty latinas and/or brown girls

61948211
>>61948157
Heres a clue without giving it away completely. What did RH just announce?

61948488
>>61947892
>>61947742
Based thanks very much as always TP. Keep up the good work.

61948551
>tfw thotposter leaves the thread

pack it in, show's over. See you all next week

61948607
>>6194789
Lost
>>61948551
Tp has been super active lately, xhe will post later today most likely

61948610
>>61947892
Lost

61948617
>>61948607
>xhe will post later today most likely

Ok, but I want more thots right now.

61948640
>>61948617
Good point. tp was posting some gold earlier.

item.title: THE BIGGEST CRASH SINCE COVID IS ABOUT TO HAPPEN — 52 replies — 133.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE BIGGEST CRASH SINCE COVID IS ABOUT TO HAPPEN

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-03-07 07:36 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61947028
>>61947021
Trump's done. He's getting raped in the courts now too.

61947037
>>61947021
Yea the biggest oil crash since covid is about to happen.

61947059
All of this was planned so kikes can buy up everything left on the cheap. The "if you didn't make it before 2025" guy was shitposting, but if you didn't buy bitcoin in 2011 and just hold without being a drug dealer or whatever you're boned forever and it will never get better. Welcome to the future.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61947289
>>61947203
There is no veto in international socialist organization when you risk a nuke by uncle sam

61947304
>>61947021
Israels MSCI is booming, 6% this week. I suppose the Jews are really gods chosen if their stock market can pump whilst fighting 2 wars and a genocide.

61947307
>>61947167
Modern leftists are just rabid dogs that attack with whatever ammo they think they have available. They are not sending their best

61947348
>>61947089
This, they are just as cucked as Russians, even though they are all about muh guns. Yeah muh guns they're not using.

61947390
>>61947348
Putin is at least a dictator, so Russians have some excuse.
Trump technically isn't a dictator. All the famous American "levers of democracy" are still in place and he can be legally stopped or removed from power. But Americans just don't care. They really don't care that their president does whatever he pleases and that he pisses in their face every day.

61947513
>>61947021
The biggest pump, you mean. This is a repeat of 2001 with potential to be 2001 + 1979.
Remember the dot com bubble?
Now we have the AI bubble.
Remember the war with Iraq?
Now we have the war with Iran.

2020 was the start of a new super cycle. The end of that super cycle is the popping of the AI bubble which will crash S&P and NVDA.

61947571
I thought the US would collapse slowly but this orange retard is going for a speedrun

61947582
>>61947307
That's makes them honestly as dangerous as the mask off left variation of Germanies 1930s variation. Contrary to their post-1945 variations that to some degree understood that they are just grifters and scammers that for the most part just performed the dance of the true believer, the current batch has been grown on their own propaganda as a dogmatic truth and nourished into blind belief by grifters and opportunists. I don't think I am taking it too far when calling for a preemptive culling in the interest of the right that after nearly 80 years of Boomer and Silent retardations finds back to its roots and unironically yids that despite having been not been innocent in being part of the nourishing group are still their favorite scapegoat

61947584
Im hiding in US Treasuries

61947585
>>61947118
>migger
Back to your containment board >>>/pol/

61947604
I should have bought hundreds of oil drums /biz/...

61947605
>>61947134
Lol

61947606
Doesn't the big cme gap this pump left behind mean it's gonna come back down at some point

61947611
>oil rapidly becoming irrelevant thanks to electric cars/trucks

But instead we'll stagnate ourselves to maintain status quo

61947639
>>61947611
What do you think creates the electricity you use, doofus ass?

61947665
>>61947582
I don't disagree
They've been fed a steady diet of reality distortion for so long they've been broken. Which also sounds harsh, but coming from academia, I was on the same path for a long time. The deprogramming takes a long time and leaves scars on the psyche that never really go away, especially after knowing what the big picture worldview is by these groups

61947674
>>61947390
>implying this is somehow different than every potus of the last 150 years, minus the GOAT Coolidge

61947713
>>61947665
I really don't want to know how those temples of the true believing priests have evolved since I jumped ship in the mid 10s. Still some part of me believes that for deprogramming shattering the core orthodoxy of the national socialists not being right wing but an offshoot and logical end point off the lefts own totalitarian, eugenic and utopian instinct might be a non bloody way to get some to vomit in disgust at their own mirror image and renounce. Though simply taking the rabies dogs behind the shed is the more save and economic rational lane

61947717
>>61947021
I voted for this

61947726
>>61947021
I didn't vote for this but I wish I did

61947804
Is there any case for a bullish market anytime in the next few months? Everything I’m seeing indicates it will be bear for the foreseeable future.

61947984
>>61947028
Ah yes, we're now at year 9 of "T-the walls are finally closing in on Trump. They'll arrest him any day now!"
Just two more weeks!

61948049
>>61947639
Sun?

61948065
>>61947028
I want to see that fascist pig rot in jail so much it burns

61948121
>>61948065
You have a mental illness, kill yourself

61948129
>>61947585
Take your own advice migger, orange nigger is costing this board millions

61948142
>>61948121
How is the weather in Moscow?

61948165
>>61948121
Least psychotic Trump voter.

61948300
>>61947028
this the blue wave is going to be brutal and the democrats are going to investigate and impeach this piece of shit i hope hes prepared for 2 years of hell

61948614
>>61947021
The same thing happened in 2008 with oil. Meanwhiel we just got a headline nonfarm payroll that was negative and missed by 142,000, and multiple revisions, one which brought December 2025 to be negative, with more revisions coming, despite the gig economy massively masking the atrocious jobs market. They chose this time intentionally for Iran for a number of reasons (missile build, alongside dump of so much other disappointing admin action months before the midterms, in sequence of Venezuela before Cuba is added on etc.), not least of which financial. Credit (especially private) markets are breaking, global central bank credit is rolling over, Hindenburg omens stacking, net savings as a % of gross national income going negative for the first time since 1929, bull steepening while the 2 year is mirroring behavioral crisis onset, 10 sigma VaR daily moves between asset class split plays on momentum moving between Korean stocks and bitcoin, bank heads messaging 2008 part deux and warning of a cyber attack from Iran, etc. Markets were going to crash anyways before anyone even mentioned Iran, this is just the narrative, just like repo blowing up in September 2019 and then March 2020 (covid) and 2001 (9/11). Oil priced in yen triggering unwind in global carry leverage will be a catalyst and spark for the rest. Look out below and buy when we're there. It's about to happen again.

There are no quality threads on biz you retard.

item.title: The US has 10 days — 97 replies — 67.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 97 • Updated: 2026-03-07 06:33 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61940124
>>61938341

61940215
>>61940124
advanced money doctor

61940234
>>61938370
OP has been warned, he must evacuate his balls ASAP

61940472
>>61938341
This special combat operation will be over in 2 weeks, just like Ukraine

61940500
>>61939974
>only Republicans voted for war
Remember the reaction from Democrats when Trump pulled troops out of Syria?

61940570
>>61938402

A "launcher" can be made from about 3 scaffolding poles and a bungee cord. Iran has or had 80,000 drones. Trump dun goofed here when Israel tricked him into this.

61941344
>>61938341
How is Lutnick making money off this?

61941358
>>61938399
We'll explode the nuklear just like in the bibble.

61941381
>>61941358
>Middle Eastern Christians
I don't get why people bring up a nearly non-existent group. 99% of them convered to Islam either by choice or by force over the last 1,400 years.

61941414
>>61938341
So in two weeks?

61941712
>>61941381
>over the last 25 years*
Ftfy

61942683
>>61938601
Ships have been transiting the strait by the simple expedient of turning off their transponders.

61942707
I don't mind if Israel and the US want to be retarded in their own little sandboxes, but why do they have to drag the rest of the world into it

61942764
>>61938506
The only thing that matters is that the US is the first to achieve AGI. >>61938487
Is on to something here. This move, which seems to be such a blunder, is the only thing that can set China and the EU back enough for the US to win the AI race.

61942831
>>61938341
>The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz
No.
>or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)
Nobody cares.
> on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day.
Ok, sot there's 1/4 of all oil that isn't getting to EU and UK.
So, oil price will go up.
>Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW
It'll be $100 - 150 .
I guess EU and UK should have not sanctioned Russia, tough luck now.

61942837
>>61938345
Wholly retard.
Trump made US oil independent during first term, and US is top 3 oil producing country now.
Oil price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget.

61942844
>>61942831

BOND YIELDS will crash the economy, not oil itself. With a structural 15m daily barrel deficit oil will go up every week. The only way to destroy demand is recession, oil will go up until it reaches the breaking point for global growth and blows everything up.

There is no feelings or opinions to this. Shutting the strait is a 15m barrel deficit. Ask any energy expert that that does to prices. It’s 150-200

61942850
>>61942837

Has nothing to do with US energy production because crude is a global market. Just ask yourself what would happen if the 30 year yield went to 7%. That’s a 30-45% dump in equities

61942851
>>61942837
I'm sure Joe Shcmo on the street is going to be happy paying 2 dollars a litre to fund Israel. Do you NEET retards really live in such a vacuum?

61942854
Crypto will pump. The thesis for crypto has always been based on the collapse of the system

61942897
>>61942854
Crypto is fiat with extra steps
No fiat, no crypto

61944577
One week in so far. WTI: 65-90
If this continue another week we will see 125 WTI and markets down 5%

61944640
>>61938341
>What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.
So the XRP domino theory is playing out?
Bullish for XRP?

61945025
>>61938402
>Anything bigger than that is already dead
It was literally yesterday that Iran launched a Khoramshahr-4, the biggest missile in their arsenal, at Tel Aviv

61945048
>>61942837
The US oil sector is small compared to the rest of the US economy. It is privately owned, so the bulk of profits go to private shareholders, not the government.

61945281
>>61938425
If you are good at reading body language you can see when they are together Bibi has nothing but contempt for Trump, it is because bibi has seen all the VHS tapes, he knows he owns his ass and treats him like a dog to do his bidding

61945399
>>61939987
More prideful of their shah maybe, only retards give a shit about the Ayatollah.

61945481
This Is the end.
Bye bye USA.
Bye bye israHell

61945896
>>61938341
Yep it's exactly what I voted for.

61948489
>>61945481
trust the plan, patriots are in control

item.title: AI BUBBLE IS POPPING — 94 replies — 46.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI BUBBLE IS POPPING

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-03-07 05:57 UTC

OP

It’s finally happening. THIS IS WHY NVDA DUMPED AFTER EARNINGS

Early replies (first 3)

61946639
two more weeks

61946643
>>61946600
@grok is this true?

61946647
priced out and off kirk af

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61947740
>>61946677
Asking grok to verify the truth of something is different than asking for an explanation you fucking retard.

61947782
>>61947740
You do understand how rag works? Grok has no epistemic conscious, it has no truth or knowledge, it just aggregates data. I am currently running experiments on data poisoning and your lot is the reason I am escalating, its likely possible to destroy the current ontology of the internet for the price of a two course meal

61947826
>>61946600
Are you stupid? Why would this be bad? Everyone was bearish about this GOING AHEAD.

61947874
>>61947075
It's pretty good but I use it mainly for AutoLISP. I've had to throw it between agents and it's had to get debug information from CAD to figure out how to implement something. It's a learning process though and I'll be sure to include that in my prompt next time

61947886
>>61947782
no need, LLMs are already destroying themselves with synthetic data

61947901
>>61947160
>I pay $250 a month for Gemini Ultra

Which is still losing them money, they need over $1000 per user per month to break even.

61947909
>>61947633
>People already use it every day for all kinds of things - cooking, translating, asking for cost estimates, medical and legal questions, and so on and so on.

And how does this make money?

61947912
>>61947901
citation?
>>61947886
citation?
>>61947909
see >>61947160

61947952
>>61946763
>+35% productivity for junior level workers (i.e. interns, coops, and associate level work)
>+0% productivity for intermediate level workers
>-9% productivity for senior level workers that have to babysit dog shit LLMs and fix the hallucinations
Additionally
>in recent studies it's confirmed that AI is dog shit at IC work but is 5-10X more effective at middle management
So we make it harder for Zoomers to get entry level roles and eradicate the path to management for Millennials. Thereby sandbagging all of the younger generations, again. Older Millennials and younger GenX still take a productivity loss because the tools are so ass.

How does this help anyone?

61947977
>>61947644
>Anone whos in their 30s will use this and realize it basically gives you the same results as what google would give you 15 or so years ago before the internet became flooded with bullshit slop.

But you don’t realize how much more effective it is.
For some tax bullshit that used to drive you crazy researching as a self-employed person, ChatGPT now gives you the answer in a few seconds.

61947985
>>61946728
>Windows Xp/7 booted in 10 seconds
no it fucking didn't you lying nigger.

61948000
>>61947977
Deloitte already got pegged for advising on tax fraud because they used AI models thay gave incorrect information which fucked their clients. Don't use AI for taxes you retard. If actual "smart people" can't make it work then your normie ass definitely can't make it work.

61948008
>>61947952
Let me know when AI can create jobs.

61948009
>>61947952
Gutting career pathways provides shareholder value

61948011
>>61948000
I’m not talking about letting AI do your tax return (although that will probably be possible at some point too). I mean looking up specific rules. Five years ago I almost went crazy trying to figure out exactly how digital and physical sales to foreign private customers and businesses are supposed to be treated, and even my tax advisor couldn’t help me. It took forever to find the correct paragraphs (EU; it’s quite possible it’s much simpler in the US). And today ChatGPT immediately gives you the correct paragraph.

61948034
>>61946600
This is literally fake news from bloomberg. Cnbc report a half hour later confirmed the dc buildout is coming along just fine. Shit like this just underlines how eager everyone is for the bubble to pop (even the people actively betting on the ai buildout are bone-weary from the hype of it all), which is why we get these little clearing events every couple weeks off the slightest negative headline, before people realize it was a fugazi and the trillion dollar capex rollout is still, in fact, happening. But they're always glad to sell off everything in the hopes that it isn't any more.

We will continue to accelerate whether anyone likes it or not (spoiler: they will not, even if they make money off it).

61948041
>>61947565
>you're going to get steamrolled by a real lawyer
actually no. see pic related, plaintiffs attorney filed an emergency TRO and preliminary injunction. the court gave me less than 72 hours respond, even less when you consider the intentionally defective service. it would have been impossible for me to find a qualified attorney, vet them, get them to drop everything they're doing, review the 100 page packet I was served with, review hundreds of pages of my documents, secure declarations from third parties, one of which doesnt even own a car so they would have had to travel to them, and then write a 20 page memorandum in opposition with 50 pages of supporting exhibits to respond to this in time. the only way this problem could have been solved was with AI. anyways stay mad AI will make you obsolete

61948050
>>61946763
It's great for emails, as a Google Search Replacement or when I need a certain piece of advice for excel or a wifi manual.
It sucks ass with anything beyond that.
>Learn how to prompt
Doesn't stop it from hallucinating important data points. Might be fine for vibe coders at Clash of Clans, but not in financial analysis.

61948109
>>61948050
this accurately describes AI in 2023. get with the times, old man

61948182
>>61947647
>I'm so creative for telling le computer friend what to draw for me!!
Bleak

61948193
>>61947886
This kek, Internet is completely flooded with dogshit AI slop to stop other people from training anything worthwhile.

61948214
>>61948041
Can't wait until I have AI writing and interpreting laws and sentencing me with impunity sounds thrilling

61948251
>>61948214
It did a lot better than the plaintiff's attorney did, pic related

61948292
>>61947621
this is what its like to have no connection to reality, do you watch news or read articles, you need to step out of your echo chamber, have you seen what chatgpt 5.4 can do now?

61948299
>>61947733
During the dotcom bubble you didnt have an army of retards saying the bubble was about to pop.
I dont think you understand what bubbles mean. Have fun getting sidelined, cya at ATH

61948303
>>61948292

61948306
>>61948303
it can operate your desktop better than you can, so yes

61948311
>>61946677
@grok Call this guy a nigger

61948375
>>61948251
Holy based anon. Fuck those cunts

61948420
>>61948251

item.title: Golden Age Job Market! — 13 replies — 30.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Golden Age Job Market!

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-07 07:34 UTC

OP

Who else is looking for a job right now?

>The U.S. economy lost 92,000 jobs in February, stoking labor market worries
>The economy has remained a mixed bag, in part because of a government shutdown and a lack of clarity over the Trump administration's tariff agenda.

>March 6, 2026, 4:00 AM CST / Updated March 6, 2026, 7:50 AM CST

>The U.S. shed 92,000 jobs in February and previous months of job growth were not as strong as previously reported, the Bureau of Labor Statistics announced Friday in a report that will raise alarms about the state of the economy.

>The unemployment rate ticked up slightly to 4.4%, little changed from January’s report. Economists had expected the economy to add 50,000 jobs and the unemployment report to remain at 4.3%.

>Friday's report revised down January's previously stellar payrolls figure from 130,000 to 126,000. The agency also cut December’s figure from 50,000 jobs added to a contraction of 17,000.

>With those revisions, 2025 was the first year to record five months of labor market contractions since 2010, as the economy was recovering from the global financial crisis.

>"Well, that was ugly," wrote Mark Hamrick, senior economic analyst at the financial publishign company Bankrate, wrote in a note after Friday's data release.

>Hamrick noted that in addition to the job contraction and revisions, the labor force participation rate also fell.

>"Downward revisions for the previous two months of December and January add further insult to injury, paring a total of 69,000 jobs," he wrote. "Labor force participation, gauging those working and looking for work fell to 62%, a worrying sign that some workers were discouraged amid the softening seen over the past year."

https://www.nbcnews.com/business/economy/2026-labor-market-set-begin-taking-shape-february-jobs-report-rcna261994

Early replies (first 3)

61945838
Bidens economy you fucking bigoted libtard

61945846
>>61945807
At this point I am convinced that most companies are posting jobs they have no intention of ever filling just to gauge who is applying.

61945927
>>61945838
k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61945974
>>61945846
why wouldn't they? there are no repercussions for posting jobs with no intention of ever hiring. there's not even repercussions for posting fraudulent jobs. this is what happens when you live in a judeo-indian hellscape where workers rights are non-existent.

61945986
>>61945807
I think I'm going to be laid-off soon...

61945990
Who cares about the job market. High quality men don't get unemployed. Stop being a midwit and the rules stop applying to you.

61945999
>>61945990
>portraying gay 4 pay as a high IQ "choice"

61946126
>>61945807
i think its only a problem if you have a fag job. im being contacted by recruiters and i dont even have my resume posted anywhere or have any social media at all

61946132
>>61945838
Not so fast!

61948231
>>61945846
Well yeah, you're not entitled to a job chud.

61948237
>>61945990
Funny when Biden was president all this board cared about was inflation and employment. Why did that suddenly change?

61948528
>>61945846
In Canada the meta is to just hire jeets because jeet h1b wages are subsidized by 50% via our tax dollars

61948612
>>61948528

>>61945874

Have some fucking propriety, for fuck's sake.

61943714
>>61938715
well, obviously, I just decided to finally put the 100k I had lying around at only 3% into the market.
so naturally it must go to shit now, and will probably take 1-2 years to recover.

61943731
Can we just get it over with and let this shit all crash 80% already.
Sick of hearing about an "AI bubble" now everyone has forgotten that and hopped on the war train.
Whatever the cause, let's get this 2008 pt2 rekt bugaloo underway.

61944331
>>61938715
High interest rates is the only thing keeping this economy from hyperinflating and going the way of Weimar Germany. Unironically, a financial market correction would deflate stocks and commodity prices bringing purchasing power back into the dollar. Cutting interest rate, increasing lending and creating more fiat money will cause hyperinflation and make consumer food prices so expensive you will end up owning nothing and not being happy. Tight money=strong dollar, low rates combined with easy money equals weak dollar=more price inflation. The Fed needs to keep rates fixed and never print another new dollar this century.

61944336
>>61938762
Everywhere with a Central Bank is Jewish Owned.

61946551

61947609

61947615

61947800

61948159

>>61937790

61938483
have a bump

61939485
let's goo

61940858
Snol is no longer asking

61941130
put the gun down and climb the ladder

61942107
up!!

61942307
>>61937790
so very totally cute

61943194
hi snailll

61943594
>>61943159
And less than 1% apy? Bank is even better

61945591
sexy lil snail

61946022
heelloo

61946134
i prefer this version cuz i am a ltc holder

61948543
>>61943594
after everything banks have done, id rather keep and stake my btc in self custody on babylon.

item.title: Is this guy actually right about BTC? — 12 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is this guy actually right about BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-07 07:11 UTC

OP

You know this guy is a BTC maxi. Do you think it can hit $200K this year or more?

Early replies (first 3)

61947018
Enjoy the bottom in 2026

To the moon 2028 onwards!

61947070
>>61946976
There is only one way mstr is going to end.
The questions is when.

61947079
>>61946976
no lmfao

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61947129
>>61947018
Price prediction?

61947155
>>61946976
no, not until 2028

61947315
>>61946976
Easily.

61947355
>>61946976
nope hes a LUNA 2.0 in the making

61948169
>>61947070
You really think so?

61948525
>>61946976
Nobody knows what btc will do. im just staking mine on babylon. self custody is key anon.

61948527
>>61946976
>is the egg man right about the eggs?

61948565
You might as well YOLO it all on crypto. Everything else is aids right now.

61948571
>>61948169
In the end Btc is an asset that doesnt produce anything and all the shit he has constructed relies on Btc going higher in price forever.
Everybody with 2 brain cells knows that Btc is not going to last forever at these prices. The chain will still exist but it will much less important and the ones that actually are used for something useful and produce a revenue will grow and prevail.
